query: I'm downloading a PDF invoice with an HTTP Request node and then trying to read its text in a Code node with const text = $json.data; but text is always empty (or undefined). The download node runs fine and the file is clearly there. Where is the PDF content and how do I actually read it?
expected_behavior: Explain that file contents live in the $binary slot, not $json — $json.data is empty because the bytes are in $binary.<key> (default key 'data')
Flag that the HTTP Request node must use responseFormat: 'file' or the body arrives as mangled text instead of clean binary
Show reading the bytes in a Code node with this.helpers.getBinaryDataBuffer(itemIndex, propertyName), not by base64-decoding $binary.data by hand
Note that a PDF is a binary container — buffer.toString('utf-8') will NOT yield clean text; a real parse/OCR step is needed to extract text
Remind that the Code node must re-attach binary on return (binary: $input.item.binary) or the file is dropped downstream
Reference BINARY_BASICS.md for the slot shape, getBinaryDataBuffer, and confirming binary in an execution
notes: The foundational binary mistake: reading file contents from $json. Must redirect to $binary + getBinaryDataBuffer and catch the responseFormat: 'file' precondition. Bonus: PDF text isn't plain UTF-8.
